deep_dive.title: LiteTopK: Exploiting the Curse of Dimensionality for a Fused Indexer-TopK Kernel in Long-Context Sparse Attention
deep_dive.abstract: Indexer-TopK, the operation to compute the scores and select the top-k candidates, is widely used by sparse attention kernels in large language models and vector retrieval in recommendation systems and vector databases. However, existing GPU-based Indexer-TopK kernels like DeepSeek Sparse Attention (DSA) remain inefficient due to excessive global memory traffic, costly synchronization, and prohibitive memory overhead. In this work, we exploit the curse of dimensionality in high-dimensional spaces, where distances between high-dimensional vectors tend to concentrate within a narrow range, to design LITETOPK, a novel and efficient fused Indexer-TopK kernel. LITETOPK first samples a small subset of data to estimate query-data score ranges, then uses these estimates to partition candidate results into bins online. This organization allows the LITETOPK kernel to maintain a tight approximate threshold, write back only promising candidates, reduce unnecessary I/O, substantially lower memory overhead, and still preserve exact Top-k correctness. Experimental results show that LITETOPK accelerates the prefill stage of GLM 5.2 by 1.2x in real-world deployment scenarios while incurring lower memory overhead.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: fused Indexer-TopK kernel for long-context sparse attention
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 用于长上下文稀疏注意力的融合索引器-TopK内核
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 用于长上下文稀疏注意力的融合索引器-TopK内核
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 用于长上下文稀疏注意力的融合索引器-TopK内核
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: LiteTopK exploits the curse of dimensionality to design a fused Indexer-TopK kernel for sparse attention, reducing global memory traffic and synchronization overhead, achieving speedup over DeepSeek Sparse Attention (DSA).
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 长上下文稀疏注意力中的索引器-TopK操作存在全局内存流量大、同步开销高的效率瓶颈。本文利用高维空间距离集中现象，设计了LiteTopK融合内核，通过先采样估计分数分布再自适应选择阈值，显著降低了内存访问和同步成本。实验显示，在多种场景下，LiteTopK相比DeepSeek稀疏注意力实现了明显加速。该工作为长上下文Transformer的高效推理提供了关键优化。

deep_dive.matched_query_text: long context attention with sparse patterns

deep_dive.title: Full-Pipeline Inference Optimization for MiMo-V2.5 Series: Pushing Hybrid SWA Efficiency to the Limit
deep_dive.abstract: We present a full-pipeline inference optimization for the MiMo-V2.5 model family, which combines Hybrid Sliding Window Attention (Hybrid SWA), sparse Mixture-of-Experts (MoE), and multimodal encoders. While Hybrid SWA can ideally reduce both attention compute and KVCache storage significantly compared to Full Attention, realizing these gains in production requires substantial engineering effort. We systematically optimize the KVCache system with layerwise prefetch, SWA-aware prefix cache trees, and specialized placement strategies, achieving strict $O(W)$ SWA storage and high cache hit rates. We further build GCache, a high-performance distributed cache infrastructure with RDMA-optimized networking, and develop a KVCache-affinity router to reduce computation while preserving load balancing. We also optimize for multimodal inputs, including GPU image preprocessing, parallel video decoding, and multimodal cache sharing. Together, these optimizations constitute the first large-scale LLM serving system in production that efficiently covers the Hybrid SWA + MoE + multimodal composite architecture.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: KV cache optimization for Hybrid Sliding Window Attention
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 面向混合滑动窗口注意力的KVCache优化
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 面向混合滑动窗口注意力的KVCache优化
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 面向混合滑动窗口注意力的KVCache优化
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Optimizes KV cache for Hybrid SWA in MiMo-V2.5, achieving O(W) storage via layerwise prefetch and SWA-aware prefix cache trees, with high cache hit rates.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对MiMo-V2.5系列中混合滑动窗口注意力（Hybrid SWA）的推理优化，提出全流水线优化方案。系统性地优化了KVCache，包括层预取、SWA感知前缀缓存树和专用放置策略，实现严格的O(W)存储和高缓存命中率。此外构建了GCache高性能分布式缓存基础设施。实验表明该方法显著降低了KVCache存储并提升了推理效率，为混合SWA模型的高效部署提供了工程实践指导。

deep_dive.title: Adaptive Filtering of the KV Cache: Diagnosing and Correcting Structural-Role Bias in LLM Inference
deep_dive.abstract: Attention-based KV cache eviction (H2O and its descendants) compresses the memory-constrained state of a long-context model by ranking tokens on accumulated attention mass, treated here as signal energy, and keeping the heaviest. On schema-dense input streams such as nested JSON, this score acts as a non-stationary filter that disproportionately retains noise: a non-content sink role (delimiters or whitespace) carries an order of magnitude more energy than any content role, and structural KEY tokens are over-retained at roughly 1.8x the rate of the answer-carrying VALUE tokens, collapsing exact-match accuracy from 88% to 0% at a 5% budget as the signal-to-noise ratio of the retained state degrades. A counterfactual experiment establishes that suppressing KEY tokens is the best deployable filter. Our retraining-free, role-conditional allocation over SnapKV's windowed score, governed by a single tuned hyperparameter, closes 63-98% of the H2O gap at sub-20% budgets and, at higher budgets, modestly matches or exceeds full-cache accuracy -- a small, seed-sensitive denoising effect (borderline significant at B=0.50; not distinguishable from zero at B=0.30 over four seeds). A 15 MB linear role probe supplies these labels at negligible inference cost, though matching parser-level downstream accuracy remains open.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: KV cache compression via adaptive filtering of structural-role bias
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 通过自适应过滤结构角色偏差进行KV缓存压缩
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 通过自适应过滤结构角色偏差进行KV缓存压缩
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 通过自适应过滤结构角色偏差进行KV缓存压缩
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper diagnoses a structural-role bias in attention-based KV cache eviction methods like H2O, which over-retains noise tokens and collapses accuracy, and proposes an adaptive filtering method to correct it.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文诊断了基于注意力的KV缓存驱逐方法（如H2O）中的结构角色偏差，该偏差导致噪声令牌被过度保留，准确率大幅下降。作者提出一种自适应过滤方法来修正偏差，在保持压缩的同时恢复长上下文推理的准确性。

deep_dive.title: VisCo: Leveraging Large Language Models as Intrinsic Encoders for Visual Token Compression
deep_dive.abstract: Vision-language models (VLMs) process large numbers of visual tokens, resulting in substantial inference latency and memory overhead. This has motivated extensive research on visual token compression. While training-free strategies rely on heuristic metrics and suffer significant performance degradation under high compression ratios, many training-based methods introduce external compression modules that force the VLM backbone to adapt, incurring substantial retraining cost and compromising VLMs' priors. Effective visual token compression hinges on strong information encoding, a capability already present in pretrained VLMs but underutilized by existing approaches. Motivated by this, we propose VisCo, a training-efficient self-compression framework that reuses the pretrained VLM itself as an intrinsic compressor. VisCo is a parameter-sharing autoencoder that compresses visual information using a small set of memory tokens and transfers hierarchical information from encoding to decoding. Experiments show that VisCo surpasses prior methods across all evaluated compression ratios, with larger gains under more aggressive compression, and remains stable even in the extreme single-token setting. Moreover, when combined with the original visual tokens, the learned memory tokens can even improve the base model, suggesting that VisCo captures complementary representations beyond compression.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: visual token compression for VLMs using LLM intrinsic encoding
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 利用LLM内在编码进行视觉令牌压缩
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 利用LLM内在编码进行视觉令牌压缩
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 利用LLM内在编码进行视觉令牌压缩
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: VisCo leverages the intrinsic encoding capability of pretrained LLMs to compress visual tokens in VLMs without external modules, achieving high compression ratios with minimal retraining.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: VisCo利用预训练VLM自身的内在编码能力进行视觉令牌自压缩，无需引入外部压缩模块。该方法训练效率高，仅需少量微调，即可在高压缩比下保持甚至提升下游任务性能，避免了传统方法对骨干网络的破坏性适应。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: VisCo利用预训练VLM自身的内在编码能力进行视觉令牌自压缩，无需引入外部压缩模块。该方法训练效率高，仅需少量微调，即可在高压缩比下保持甚至提升下游任务性能，避免了传统方法对骨干网络的破坏性适应。

deep_dive.title: Realizable N:M Sparse Transformer Inference via Search-Kernel Co-Design
deep_dive.abstract: Vision Transformers (ViTs) achieve strong accuracy but incur high inference latency. Semi-structured N:M sparsity can reduce arithmetic cost, yet its theoretical savings often fail to translate into proportional end-to-end speedups on modern GPUs. This mismatch arises because deployment latency depends not only on arithmetic reduction but also on execution regularity and hardware scheduling under sparsity. Achieving practical acceleration, therefore, requires coordinated design across sparse execution and sparsity configuration. To this end, we propose a hardware-software co-design framework for N:M sparse ViT inference. On the hardware side, we design MD-SpMM, an N:M sparse CUDA kernel that reorganizes sparse GEMM into micro-dense, Tensor-Core-aligned dataflow and uses inference-aware adaptive parallelism to sustain utilization. On the software side, we perform layer-wise sparsity search under explicit end-to-end latency budgets using a three-stage heuristic search with constraint relaxation to avoid premature convergence and enable deployment-aware sparsity allocation. Experiments on multiple ViT/Swin models and GPU platforms show that the framework achieves over 2.2x latency speedup while maintaining comparable accuracy and delivering superior accuracy under the same latency constraint. The source code is publicly available at https://github.com/liuganhuo/realizable-nm-sparse-transformer.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: N:M sparse CUDA kernel for efficient ViT inference
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 设计N:M稀疏CUDA内核加速ViT推理
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 设计N:M稀疏CUDA内核加速ViT推理
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 设计N:M稀疏CUDA内核加速ViT推理
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes hardware-software co-design for N:M sparse ViT inference, including MD-SpMM kernel that reorganizes sparse GEMM into micro-dense Tensor-Core-aligned operations.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对Vision Transformer推理延迟高的问题，提出N:M稀疏推理的软硬件协同设计框架。设计了MD-SpMM内核，将稀疏矩阵乘法重组为微稠密张量核心对齐运算，使理论加速转化为实际端到端加速。实验在多种模型上实现了显著的推理速度提升，证明了N:M稀疏在实际部署中的可行性，为稀疏注意力在视觉Transformer中的应用奠定了基础。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对Vision Transformer推理延迟高的问题，提出N:M稀疏推理的软硬件协同设计框架。设计了MD-SpMM内核，将稀疏矩阵乘法重组为微稠密张量核心对齐运算，使理论加速转化为实际端到端加速。实验在多种模型上实现了显著的推理速度提升，证明了N:M稀疏在实际部署中的可行性，为稀疏注意力在视觉Transformer中的应用奠定了基础。

quick_skim.title: When Structured Sparse Autoencoders Learn Consistent Concepts Across Modalities
quick_skim.abstract: Sparse autoencoders (SAEs) have emerged as a promising technique for mechanistic interpretability by learning a set of sparse latent features in large models, each of which encodes a distinct concept. However, in vision-language models (VLMs), vanilla SAEs struggle to learn modality-consistent concepts, with concepts often exhibiting fragmented coverage (i.e., disjoint regions) in the visual modality. To address this challenge, we propose a Structured Sparse AutoEncoder ($S^2AE$) that enforces concept consistency from both semantic and spatial perspectives in the visual modality. Specifically, we group image patches based on Transformer attention similarity and spatial proximity, and introduce a structured sparsity regularization when training the vanilla SAE. The regularization consists of exclusive sparsity for inter-group concept disentanglement and group sparsity for intra-group concept consistency, which drives the latent neurons by SAEs to specialize in distinct, semantically grounded concepts. Evaluated on the \texttt{Qwen2.5-VL-7B-Instruct} model, the method achieves 6.06% average improvement in semantic alignment (mIoU) and 60.81 in representational efficiency (lower l0 norm) while maintaining near-perfect reconstruction fidelity with an Explained Variance above 99%. Cross-modal analysis further demonstrates that $S^2AE$ enhances neuronal monosemanticity by this visual structural prior, achieving a 3.08% average gain in semantic consistency and a 2.37% average gain in monosemanticity scores for both modalities of multimodal features, thereby fostering more coherent and disentangled representations.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: structured sparse autoencoder for VLM concept consistency
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 用于视觉语言模型的概念一致性的结构化稀疏自编码器
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 用于视觉语言模型的概念一致性的结构化稀疏自编码器
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 用于视觉语言模型的概念一致性的结构化稀疏自编码器
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Structured Sparse AutoEncoder (S2AE) enforces concept consistency across vision and language modalities by grouping image patches via attention similarity and spatial proximity, improving interpretability in VLMs.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 视觉语言模型中普通稀疏自编码器难以学习跨模态一致的概念，视觉模态常出现碎片化覆盖。本文提出结构化稀疏自编码器（S2AE），通过注意力相似性和空间邻近性对图像块分组，并引入结构化稀疏正则化训练，强制概念在语义和空间上一致。实验表明，S2AE比普通SAE更好地学习了跨模态一致的概念，提高了可解释性。该工作为多模态模型的可解释性提供了有效工具。

quick_skim.title: Kaleido: Algorithm-Hardware Co-Design for Video Diffusion Transformers by Exploiting Latent Space Correlations
quick_skim.abstract: Video diffusion transformers (vDiTs) generate high quality video but introduce extremely high compute cost due to the long diffusion timesteps and self attention computation. As diffusion timesteps are reduced, the computation cost of self attention becomes the dominant bottleneck. Existing acceleration approaches largely inherit sparse attention techniques from large language models, which fail to consider the unique spatiotemporal correlation of video data.   This paper presents Kaleido, an algorithm hardware codesign that accelerates all operations in vDiTs by exploiting channel-wise spatiotemporal correlations in latent space. Based on this insight, we propose a lightweight channelwise reuse algorithm that skips redundant computations by reusing partial results while preserving higher generative quality than prior methods (>17 dB). To efficiently support this algorithm, we design a systolic array like accelerator with reconfigurable processing elements and a lightweight data dispatcher to mitigate irregular sparsity and data access patterns introduced by our reuse algorithm. Evaluations across three mainstream vDiT models show that Kaleido achieves up to 5.9x speedup and 16.0x energy savings over state of the art accelerators.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: sparse attention techniques from LLMs applied to video diffusion transformers
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 将LLM中的稀疏注意力技术应用于视频扩散Transformer
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 将LLM中的稀疏注意力技术应用于视频扩散Transformer
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 将LLM中的稀疏注意力技术应用于视频扩散Transformer
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Kaleido accelerates video diffusion transformers by exploiting channel-wise spatiotemporal correlations in latent space, using a lightweight channelwise reuse algorithm that skips computation, building on sparse attention ideas.

quick_skim.title: Extending LLM Context via Associative Recurrent Memory
quick_skim.abstract: Extending the context length of large language models (LLMs) is critical for many real-world applications, yet standard transformers remain constrained by quadratic compute and linear memory scaling. In this work, we investigate the Associative Recurrent Memory Transformer (ARMT) as a practical approach for enabling long-context processing in LLMs, constant memory scaling, and better efficiency. We make three main contributions. First, we construct two domain-specific long-context datasets designed to evaluate realistic workloads, focusing on narrow-domain fine-tuning scenarios. Second, we propose a comprehensive training recipe for ARMT-based context extension, combining continued pre-training, synthetic long-context data generation, curriculum learning, and selective integration of associative memory into chosen model layers. Third, we present an extensive experimental study demonstrating that ARMT-augmented models: (i) process inputs well beyond their original context limits without degrading performance relative to in-limit baselines; (ii) generalize more effectively to out-of-distribution context lengths; and (iii) need 30% less FLOPs while preserving baseline performance within the original context window.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: extending LLM context with constant memory via associative recurrent memory
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过关联循环记忆以恒定内存扩展LLM上下文
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过关联循环记忆以恒定内存扩展LLM上下文
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过关联循环记忆以恒定内存扩展LLM上下文
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Associative Recurrent Memory Transformer (ARMT) enables long-context processing in LLMs with constant memory scaling, using a training recipe with synthetic data and curriculum learning.

quick_skim.title: Forgetful Attention: A Trainable Support-Vector Memory with Certified Selection and Exact Unlearning
quick_skim.abstract: Attention can be viewed as an online learner over context, yet existing test-time memories cannot certify that dropping a token leaves outputs unchanged or delete its influence outright. We introduce Support Vector Attention (SV-Attention), a max-margin memory whose weights are support coefficients of a one-class SVM with fixed box parameter C. Its active-set partition gives reserve tokens exactly zero weight, certifying output-preserving eviction; a reversible incremental solver deletes a token to recover the state produced by retraining without it under the same C. In fp64 experiments, decrement and refit recover identical partitions whenever the optimum is unique, and their decision functions match to a median deviation of about 10^-9 (10^-13 on learned keys); the 10^-2 worst case is confined to ill-conditioned duplicates and remains below coefficient decay in every regime. The exact path reuses the maintained KKT inverse in a custom backward. Training uses a separate stabilized batched approximation and does not carry the exact-deletion certificate; it reaches 9,125 tokens/s on a 3.22M-parameter model, while remaining 35.8 times slower than an MPS softmax reference. At matched budgets, certified selection reaches 0.86 vs. 0.32 rare-item recall and retains 0.80 vs. 0.05 deterioration hours on real MIMIC-IV streams. We also demonstrate surgical forgetting, exact editing, patient-record deletion, and a forgettable retrieval memory over real sentence embeddings. On enwik8, the hybrid obtains 2.178 BPC vs. 2.383 for a matched-state sliding-window Transformer across seven seeds (8.6% paired improvement, p=0.001); a three-seed TinyStories result is directionally positive but not significant (p=0.057).
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: efficient attention with certified token eviction
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 具有可验证令牌驱逐的高效注意力机制
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 具有可验证令牌驱逐的高效注意力机制
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 具有可验证令牌驱逐的高效注意力机制
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Support Vector Attention uses a max-margin memory with certified output-preserving eviction and exact unlearning via reversible solver, enabling efficient attention with provable deletion.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有注意力机制无法保证删除某个令牌后输出不变或完全消除其影响。本文提出支持向量注意力（SV-Attention），其权重为一类SVM的支持系数，通过活动集划分使保留令牌权重为零，从而可验证地保留输出；并利用可逆增量求解器实现精确遗忘。实验证明在fp64下与重新训练结果一致。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有注意力机制无法保证删除某个令牌后输出不变或完全消除其影响。本文提出支持向量注意力（SV-Attention），其权重为一类SVM的支持系数，通过活动集划分使保留令牌权重为零，从而可验证地保留输出；并利用可逆增量求解器实现精确遗忘。实验证明在fp64下与重新训练结果一致。

quick_skim.title: Inhibited Self-Attention: Sharpening Focus in Vision Transformers
quick_skim.abstract: Vision Transformers (ViTs) have demonstrated remarkable performance in computer vision tasks. However, their self-attention mechanism often diffuses focus across background regions, relying on spurious correlations rather than object-relevant cues. Inspired by inhibitory mechanisms observed in biological vision systems, we propose the Inhibited Self-Attention (ISA), a novel self-attention that integrates inhibitory signals to enhance feature selectivity and suppress spurious responses. In contrast to conventional self-attention, which relies solely on positive attention values due to softmax normalization, our approach retains and utilizes negative attention scores to suppress irrelevant features and sharpen focus on objects of interest. Experiments across multiple datasets, including ImageNet-1k and COCO, and several robustness benchmarks demonstrate that ISA enhances object-centric selectivity, reduces shortcut reliance, and improves out-of-distribution generalization. Our analysis of relevance maps confirms that ViTs with ISA exhibit sharper, more localized focus on object-relevant regions while reducing distractions from non-relevant (background) features, enabling more reliable models. We release our code at https://github.com/prdvanderwal/inhibited-self-attention
authors: Peter R. D. van der Wal
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: novel self-attention variant with inhibitory signals for sharper focus
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出抑制性自注意力，增强特征选择性
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出抑制性自注意力，增强特征选择性
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出抑制性自注意力，增强特征选择性
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Inhibited Self-Attention (ISA) that uses negative attention scores to suppress irrelevant features, improving focus in Vision Transformers.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对Vision Transformer中自注意力扩散到背景区域的问题，受生物视觉抑制机制启发，提出抑制性自注意力（ISA）。该方法保留并利用负注意力分数来抑制无关特征，增强对目标对象的聚焦。实验证明ISA在多个视觉任务上提升了性能，为设计更有效的自注意力机制提供了新思路，展示了非稀疏注意力改进的有效性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对Vision Transformer中自注意力扩散到背景区域的问题，受生物视觉抑制机制启发，提出抑制性自注意力（ISA）。该方法保留并利用负注意力分数来抑制无关特征，增强对目标对象的聚焦。实验证明ISA在多个视觉任务上提升了性能，为设计更有效的自注意力机制提供了新思路，展示了非稀疏注意力改进的有效性。

quick_skim.title: Attention-Free and Lightweight Token Reduction for Efficient Vision-Language Models
quick_skim.abstract: Vision-Language Models (VLMs) have achieved strong performance in multimodal understanding, yet remain challenging to deploy on resource-constrained edge devices due to the substantial computational overhead of processing numerous visual tokens. Token reduction is a promising direction for accelerating VLMs inference, but existing approaches either rely on attention maps that are incompatible with modern acceleration frameworks or depend on computationally intensive pairwise similarity comparisons, which undermine scalability and negate their practical benefits in deployment. In this paper, we propose an attention-free and lightweight token reduction framework as a plug-and-play module for VLMs, which preserves both important and diverse tokens to produce a compact visual representation. First, to enable attention-free importance estimation, we adopt an information-theoretic perspective and quantify token information using a novel entropy-based criterion, retaining those with more expressive and less degenerate feature representations. Second, to ensure diverse visual coverage in a lightweight manner, we introduce a transformation-induced consistency signal where similar tokens yield similar signals, such that sorting by this signal places similar tokens close to each other and enables stride-based selection to produce a diverse token set. Extensive experiments across multiple VLMs benchmarks demonstrate that our framework achieves a favorable accuracy-efficiency trade-off, maintaining competitive performance under aggressive compression.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: attention-free token reduction for efficient vision-language models
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 面向高效视觉语言模型的无注意力令牌减少
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 面向高效视觉语言模型的无注意力令牌减少
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 面向高效视觉语言模型的无注意力令牌减少
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper proposes a lightweight, attention-free token reduction method for VLMs that preserves important and diverse tokens, achieving efficient inference without modifying backbone.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出一种轻量级、无注意力的令牌减少框架，作为视觉语言模型（VLM）的即插即用模块。该方法在保留重要且多样令牌的同时，避免了传统注意力地图或成对相似性计算的开销，显著加速推理，适用于资源受限的边缘设备。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文提出一种轻量级、无注意力的令牌减少框架，作为视觉语言模型（VLM）的即插即用模块。该方法在保留重要且多样令牌的同时，避免了传统注意力地图或成对相似性计算的开销，显著加速推理，适用于资源受限的边缘设备。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 多模态大模型相关论文，包括理解、检索、幻觉等方向, especially work that connects or combines: multimodal understanding models; multimodal hallucination detection; multimodal reasoning tasks; papers on multimodal understanding in large language models; causes and mitigation of hallucination in multimodal models; architecture of large multimodal models combining vision and language.
